channel.title: Dotnet on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Dotnet on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: dotnet: Announcing .NET Modernization for Beginners
item.description:
Microsoft announced a free, open-source hands-on course for modernizing a legacy ASP.NET application to .NET 10 using the GitHub Copilot modernization agent. The course is step-by-step and aimed at beginners.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework July 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of July 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include version numbers, fixes, or platform-specific details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: CoreCLR Progress and the Mono Timeline for .NET MAUI
item.description:
.NET MAUI mobile apps now use CoreCLR as the only runtime in .NET 11 Preview 6. The post says the team is at this transition point and asks developers to test the change now.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 6 is now available!
item.description:
The post announces .NET 11 Preview 6 and says it includes updates across runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, F#, and container images. No specific feature details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Modernize .NET applications in the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot app now includes an interactive upgrade canvas for modernizing .NET applications. It supports the upgrade process from assessment through execution.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: MCP Beyond the Chat Window: Build Diagnostics in CI
item.description:
Microsoft’s .NET blog describes using Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools for build diagnostics in CI, including the full Binlog MCP toolset and how it runs in a GitHub Actions workflow. It also reports evaluation data on efficiency gains from this approach.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026
item.description:
Microsoft says .NET 8 and .NET 9 will both reach end of support on November 10, 2026. The post directs users to upgrade to .NET 10, which is the next LTS release.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release
item.description:
SkiaSharp 4.148.0 is the first stable release of SkiaSharp v4. The post says it covers what’s new in this release and references a live event on June 30.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows
item.description:
Microsoft describes using the WinApp CLI to add package identity and create packages for .NET desktop apps on Windows. This enables deeper Windows integration and simpler distribution.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: AI-Powered MSBuild Investigation with the Microsoft Binlog MCP Server
item.description:
Microsoft introduced the Binlog MCP Server for investigating MSBuild build failures and performance issues with AI. It provides 15 specialized tools for analyzing MSBuild binary logs through an AI assistant.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Join us for .NET Day on Agentic Modernization Livestream
item.description:
Microsoft announced a .NET Day livestream focused on agentic modernization. The post is an event announcement and does not include technical product changes or release details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 5 is now available!
item.description:
The post announces .NET 11 Preview 5 and says it includes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and other areas. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework June 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
This post is a brief recap of the June 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET at Microsoft Build 2026: Must watch sessions
item.description:
Microsoft’s Build 2026 .NET post highlights sessions covering .NET 11, union types in C#, AI building blocks, the agentic web, and .NET MAUI. It is a session roundup rather than a product release note.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Doing More with GitHub Copilot as a .NET Developer
item.description:
The post is a brief .NET Blog entry about getting more out of GitHub Copilot as a .NET developer. It points readers to easy ways to start using Copilot more effectively, but provides no technical details in the excerpt.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Give Your .NET MAUI Android Apps a Material 3 Makeover
item.description:
Material 3 support is now available for .NET MAUI apps on Android. The post explains how to enable it, what changes are included now, and what is still planned.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Announcing Agent Governance Toolkit MCP Extensions for .NET
item.description:
Microsoft announced a public preview .NET package for MCP servers that adds governance features through a single builder extension. The package includes policy enforcement, startup tool scanning, fallback governance, and response sanitization.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Improving C# Memory Safety
item.description:
C# unsafe is being redesigned to express caller-facing safety contracts instead of only enabling unsafe syntax. The change is in preview for .NET 11 and is meant to make safety obligations clearer and easier to review.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: NuGet Package Pruning: Cleaner Dependencies and Actionable Vulnerability Reports
item.description:
In .NET 10, package pruning removes platform-provided packages from the dependency graph. With transitive auditing enabled by default, projects using the new defaults see about 70% fewer transitive vulnerability reports than with the previous defaults.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Process API Improvements in .NET 11
item.description:
The post describes Process API changes in .NET 11, adding higher-level process-starting APIs, safer output capture to avoid deadlocks, control over handle inheritance, lifetime management features, and a lighter API surface based on SafeProcessHandle.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11
item.description:
In .NET 11, .NET MAUI apps on Android, iOS, and Mac Catalyst run on CoreCLR by default. This aligns MAUI with the same runtime used across the rest of .NET.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available!
item.description:
The .NET 11 Preview 4 blog post announces the preview release and notes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and related areas. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
Microsoft published a May 2026 recap of servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided text does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot Studio upgraded its WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was described as straightforward, simplified deployment, and improved performance for end users.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description:
Part 3 of the Microsoft Agent Framework series shows how to build .NET AI agents using tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows. It ties together the earlier parts into the framework’s main building blocks for agent development.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Building an AI-Powered Conference App with .NET’s Composable AI Stack
item.description:
The post describes building ConferencePulse, an AI-powered conference assistant, using .NET’s composable AI stack. It highlights Microsoft.Extensions.AI, DataIngestion, VectorData, Model Context Protocol, and Microsoft Agent Framework as the main components.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description:
The post describes how to govern MCP tool calls in .NET using the Agent Governance Toolkit. It focuses on policy-based access control, security scanning, and response sanitization to reduce risk in AI agent systems.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: VSTest is Removing its Newtonsoft.Json Dependency
item.description:
VSTest will remove its Newtonsoft.Json dependency in .NET 11 and Visual Studio 18.8. The post says it explains who is affected and how to adjust to the change.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Welcome to SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1
item.description:
SkiaSharp 4.0 Preview 1 has been released. The post also notes that Uno Platform has joined as a co-maintainer.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description:
The post introduces a .NET walkthrough for adding distributed caching with Postgres on Azure to improve performance, reduce latency, and lower load on downstream systems. It presents current caching best practices and packages them into a reusable application example.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: Combining API versioning with OpenAPI in .NET 10 applications
item.description:
The post explains how to add API versioning to .NET 10 applications and document those versions with OpenAPI. It focuses on using both together for clearer API management and documentation.

  • Source: .NET
